title: The dinner invitation
setting: Your friend Sara invites you to her family's home for dinner.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Wetsch am Friitig zu öis go Znacht ässe?
steps.en: Would you like to come to ours for dinner on Friday?

options.es: Sehr gärn! Das isch lieb, dass d'mich iiladsch.
options.en: Gladly! It's sweet of you to invite me.
options.feedback: Warm acceptance that acknowledges the kindness. Just right.
Accepts, but flatly. A little warmth honors the invitation.
Hesitant for a kind invite — if you can come, say yes warmly.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Super! Mini Eltere fröied sich, dich z'gseh.
steps.en: Great! My parents are looking forward to meeting you.

options.feedback: Exactly the polite question to ask — it's expected etiquette here.

options.en: I'm looking forward to it too!
options.feedback: Warm and natural. You could also offer to bring something.
Reads as rude about your hosts' cooking. Avoid — just express thanks.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Nu di säuber! Aber Blueme freued mini Mueter immer.
steps.en: Just yourself! But flowers always please my mother.

options.es: Denn bring ich ere Blueme. Guete Tipp!
options.en: Then I'll bring her flowers. Good tip!
options.feedback: Perfect — you took the hint graciously. That'll land beautifully.

options.es: Alles klar, merci für de Tipp.
options.en: Got it, thanks for the tip.

options.feedback: Sounds stingy about a kind gesture. A small bouquet goes a long way here.

steps.speaker: Sara
steps.es: Perfekt, denn bis Friitig am sibni!
steps.en: Perfect, then see you Friday at seven!

options.es: Bis Friitig, ich freu mich! Merci, Sara.
options.en: See you Friday, looking forward to it! Thanks, Sara.
options.feedback: Warm, grateful, personal. A model response.
A bit flat for such a warm invitation. Show you're pleased.
